description: NVIDIA Triton Inference Server — throughput, latency, GPU health, memory and cache
title: Triton Inference Server

panels.title: LLM Concurrent Requests
panels.description: Active LLM requests currently running inside the vLLM scheduler.

panels.title: Embedding Concurrent Requests
panels.description: Active embedding requests currently running inside the vLLM scheduler.

panels.title: Pending Requests (Queue Depth)
panels.description: Requests received but not yet executing. Sustained high values = capacity constrained.

panels.title: Failure Breakdown by Reason
panels.description: REJECTED=queue timeout, CANCELED=client cancel, BACKEND=model error, OTHER=uncategorized

panels.title: Inference Count vs Execution Count
panels.description: Gap between the two lines indicates batching. Inference Count / Execution Count = avg batch size.

panels.title: Pending Request Count (Queue Depth) over Time
panels.description: Requests waiting to be executed. Sustained growth = model cannot keep up with load.

panels.title: Avg End-to-End Latency per Model (ms)
panels.description: Total request duration including queue wait and compute.

panels.title: Latency Waterfall (stacked avg ms)
panels.description: Stacked view of where time is spent. Dominant queue = capacity issue. Dominant compute = model is the bottleneck.

panels.title: Avg Queue Wait Time per Model (ms)
panels.description: Time spent waiting in the scheduler queue before execution starts.

panels.title: Avg Model Compute Time per Model (ms)
panels.description: Time the model backend spends executing inference. Normalized per execution (batch).

panels.title: Avg Compute I/O Overhead per Model (ms)
panels.description: Time spent on input preprocessing and output postprocessing (outside of model execution).

panels.title: Triton Time to First Response — P50 / P90 / P99 (ms)
panels.description: Computed from nv_inference_first_response_histogram_ms. Measures end-to-end from Triton's perspective.

panels.title: vLLM Time to First Token — TTFT P50 / P90 / P99
panels.description: From vLLM backend. Covers both LLM (vllm_llms_v1:) and embedding (vllm_embeddings_v1:) models.

panels.title: Time Per Output Token — P50 / P90 / P99
panels.description: Time between consecutive generated tokens (inter-token latency). Only meaningful for LLM models, not embeddings.

panels.title: End-to-End Request Latency — P50 / P90 / P99
panels.description: Full request latency from vLLM backend. Covers both LLM and embedding models.

panels.title: Prefill & Decode Time
panels.description: Per-phase time inside vLLM: prefill processes the prompt, decode generates each output token.

panels.title: Triton Queue & Compute Latency Percentiles (µs)
panels.description: Pre-computed sliding window quantiles from Triton summary metrics. Queue wait and model compute time at P50/P99.

panels.title: vLLM Scheduler State (Running / Waiting)
panels.description: Number of requests in RUNNING and WAITING state inside the vLLM scheduler.

panels.title: vLLM KV Cache Utilization
panels.description: Percentage of KV cache blocks in use. Approaching 100% causes request queuing or preemption.

panels.title: Request Queue Time (vLLM)
panels.description: Time each request spent waiting in vLLM's internal scheduler queue before execution begins.

panels.title: Preemptions Rate
panels.description: Rate of scheduler preemptions. Each preemption evicts a running request from the KV cache due to memory pressure — a sustained rate here indicates the KV cache is too small for the current load.

panels.title: Iteration Batch Size — P50 / P90 / P99 (tokens)
panels.description: Distribution of total tokens processed per engine step (prompt + generation). Low P50 relative to max batch size indicates the GPU is under-utilised; a high P99 suggests occasional very large batches.

panels.title: Token Throughput (tokens/sec)
panels.description: Prompt tokens/sec = ingestion speed. Generation tokens/sec = output speed. Covers both LLM (vllm_llms_v1:) and embedding (vllm_embeddings_v1:) backends.

panels.title: Avg Tokens per Request
panels.description: Average prompt and generation token counts per request. Covers both LLM and embedding models.

panels.title: Request Prompt Length Distribution
panels.description: Heatmap of prompt token counts per request.

panels.title: Request Generation Length Distribution
panels.description: Heatmap of generation token counts per request.

panels.title: Max Generation Tokens in Sequence Group
panels.description: Rate of max output tokens configured per request. Indicates how far requests are allowed to generate.

panels.title: Max Tokens per Request — P50 / P90 / P99
panels.description: Distribution of the explicit max_tokens parameter set by callers. Useful for capacity planning — a rising P99 means clients are requesting longer generations.

panels.title: Prefix Cache — Tokens Cached / sec
panels.description: Rate of prompt tokens served from the prefix cache (local + external). Compare against prompt_tokens_total to derive an effective cache hit rate in tokens.

panels.title: GPU Memory Used vs Total (per GPU)

targets.expr: avg by (gpu_uuid) (nv_gpu_memory_used_bytes{gpu_uuid=~"$gpu", environment=~"$environment"}) / avg by (gpu_uuid) (nv_gpu_memory_total_bytes{gpu_uuid=~"$gpu", environment=~"$environment"}) * 100

panels.title: GPU Power Usage vs Limit (per GPU)
panels.description: Approaching the power limit risks thermal throttling.

panels.title: Cumulative Energy Consumption (per GPU)
panels.description: Total energy since Triton started. Useful for cost tracking.

panels.title: CPU Usage
panels.description: Percentage of CPU time spent in non-idle modes, averaged across all cores per instance.

panels.title: RAM Usage
panels.description: Percentage of total memory in use (total minus MemAvailable) per instance.

panels.title: Disk Availability
panels.description: Percentage of disk space still available per mount point and instance. Thresholds warn at 20% free and alert at 10% free.
